description: Reviewed dispositions for Java-vs-.NET API-surface deltas. Entries are keyed by the api_review.key values emitted into reports/api-surface-comparison.json.

entries.note: XmpBox schema JavaBean accessors and property-object overloads are represented by the C# schema property/helper surface and XML round-trip storage instead of one-for-one Java method names.

Java TRUE/FALSE constants are exposed as C# TrueValue/FalseValue constants while preserving boolean parsing and string output behavior.
Java TRUE/FALSE constants are exposed as C# TrueValue/FalseValue constants while preserving boolean parsing and string output behavior.
Java enum method is exposed as the C# CardinalityExtensions.IsArray() extension method.
Java getProperty() is exposed as GetPropertyValue() to avoid ambiguity with inherited named-property lookup in the C# type hierarchy.
Java getType() is exposed as GetTypeValue() to avoid conflict with object.GetType() and keep the PDF/A field meaning explicit.
Java annotation element type() is exposed through C# PropertyTypeAttribute.TypeName and Type properties.
Java annotation elements are exposed as C# StructuredTypeAttribute.Namespace and PreferedPrefix properties.
Java annotation elements are exposed as C# StructuredTypeAttribute.Namespace and PreferedPrefix properties.
Parser artifact for the private Java enum constructor; not a public API member to port.
Java enum predicate methods are exposed as C# boolean properties on the enum-like Types registry.
Java enum predicate methods are exposed as C# boolean properties on the enum-like Types registry.
Java enum predicate methods are exposed as C# boolean properties on the enum-like Types registry.
Version accessors are intentionally named GetVersionValue/SetVersionValue in C# to make the XMP field value explicit.
Version accessors are intentionally named GetVersionValue/SetVersionValue in C# to make the XMP field value explicit.
Java getErrorType() is exposed as the C# Type property on XmpParsingException.
Custom Java XML factory constructor is not exposed because the .NET serializer uses XmlWriter/XmlDocument settings directly.
Java DOM helper method is internal to the .NET serializer design; public serialization behavior is exposed through Serialize().
Java DOM helper method is internal to the .NET serializer design; public serialization behavior is exposed through Serialize().
Java DOM helper method is internal to the .NET serializer design; public serialization behavior is exposed through Serialize().
Java annotation is ported as C# PropertyTypeAttribute; consumers still use the idiomatic short attribute form [PropertyType].
Java annotation is ported as C# StructuredTypeAttribute; consumers still use the idiomatic short attribute form [StructuredType].
Java ByteSource overload is collapsed into .NET byte[] and RandomAccessRead parsing entry points.
Java diagnostic toString() is not preserved as a dedicated C# override; parser functionality is exposed through Parse methods.
Java Type1CharStringReader constructor shape is collapsed into parsed-sequence constructors in the .NET charstring model.
CharStringCommand helper behavior is exposed through C# extension methods; extension methods are not reflected as members on the enum type.

Java Type1CharString diagnostic/constructor shape is adapted to the simplified .NET byte/GeneralPath model.
Java protected charstring interpreter hooks are internal to the .NET renderer implementation.
Java AWT Rectangle2D bounds API is not exposed by the FontBox-only geometry layer; callers use rendered GeneralPath data.
Java protected charstring interpreter hooks are internal to the .NET renderer implementation.

Parser artifact for the public INSTANCE singleton initializer; MacRomanEncoding.INSTANCE is already present and the constructor remains private.
SubstitutingCmapLookup is an internal .NET implementation detail behind public cmap/GSUB lookup APIs.
SubstitutingCmapLookup is an internal .NET implementation detail behind public cmap/GSUB lookup APIs.
SubstitutingCmapLookup is an internal .NET implementation detail behind public cmap/GSUB lookup APIs.
Java NO_DATA_FOUND singleton is represented by IGsubData.NoDataFound on the C# interface.
Language script-name behavior is exposed as the C# LanguageExtensions.GetScriptNames() extension method.
SubstitutingCmapLookup remains internal in the .NET port; glyph substitution lookup is exposed through public TrueTypeFont/GlyphSubstitutionTable APIs.
Java public interface is ported as C# IGlyphArraySplitter using the standard interface prefix; member surface is otherwise equivalent.
Java public interface is ported as C# IGsubWorker using the standard interface prefix; member surface is otherwise equivalent.
Java public interface is ported as C# IGsubData using the standard interface prefix; NoDataFound singleton remains available on the interface.
Java public interface is ported as C# IScriptFeature using the standard interface prefix; member surface is otherwise equivalent.

FinalDecodeOptions is an immutable DEFAULT helper. The C# port keeps the helper private and exposes the same immutable DecodeOptions.DEFAULT behavior.
BruteForceParser is an adapted wrapper over the C# PDFParser stream workflow; Java recovery-state construction and bfSearchTriggered inspection are not exposed as public .NET API.
Brute-force xref recovery helpers remain parser implementation details in the adapted C# parser pipeline.

PDFXRefStream is represented as a COSStream wrapper in the C# port; xref entry/trailer mutation is handled by PDFParser/PDDocument serialization code rather than this Java builder API.

PDFXrefStreamParser returns the parsed wrapper directly; resolver population is handled by the surrounding C# parser flow instead of a Java parse(XrefTrailerResolver) overload.
XrefParser delegates to the adapted PDFParser flow; xref tables are exposed from COSDocument/XrefTrailerResolver rather than Java parseXref/getXrefTable methods.

XReferenceEntry exposes GetXReferenceType because getType conflicts with System.Object.GetType in C#.
XReferenceType is a C# enum with the Java numeric values as underlying enum values; callers use an int cast instead of getNumericValue().
